title: heteromorpha arborescens var. collina
intro: Heteromorpha arborescens var. collina, also known as the forest foxtail or woodland foxtail, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that is native to southern Africa, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, relieve pain and inflammation, and protect against infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or decoction, and can also be used in the form of a cream or ointment to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and wounds.
